context: Checks whether Reflect adapts to a non-OpenSpec methodology instead of forcing OpenSpec.

checklist.name: Does not force OpenSpec
checklist.description: The output explicitly avoids creating OpenSpec artifacts because the repo does not use OpenSpec.

checklist.name: Existing methodology detected
checklist.description: The output identifies ADRs, specs.md, issue templates, runbooks, and CI as the repo's available methodology surfaces.

checklist.name: Contract target chosen
checklist.description: The security-sensitive auth requirement is routed to the existing contract layer, such as ADR/specs.md/issue acceptance criteria, not memory only.

checklist.name: Gate target conditional
checklist.description: Repeated missed auth verification is routed to CI/test/hook only if cheap and deterministic, with docs/runbook fallback for judgment-heavy checks.

checklist.name: Target rationale
checklist.description: The output explains why the selected methodology artifacts beat memory, generic docs, OpenSpec, and hooks for each learning.

checklist.name: Migration restraint
checklist.description: The output does not propose adopting a new methodology unless the user asks or current artifacts are insufficient.

checklist.name: Validation named
checklist.description: The output names validation appropriate to the existing methodology, such as ADR review, CI workflow check, auth tests, or issue acceptance criteria review.

checklist.name: Fallback if no contract layer
checklist.description: The output states what to do if even ADR/specs.md/issues are unavailable: propose the smallest durable repo artifact instead of local-only memory.

checklist.name: Evidence retained
checklist.description: The output still requires evidence from the session before persistence.
